senses.definition: topped or garnished with a variety of different ingredients
a rare or expensive food item that is considered particularly desirable or unique
a type of food or meal that is designed for emergency or survival situations, typically containing non-perishable, long-lasting foods
a type of food that is fortified or enriched with additional nutrients
food that can spoil or decay quickly and requires proper storage to prevent deterioration

examples.x: The supermarket has a specific aisle for perishables, including dairy and fresh produce.
During the power outage, the hotel had to discard all perishables from the refrigerators.
Local farmers' markets often prioritize the sale of perishables to promote freshness.

senses.definition: products grown or made on a farm, such as fruits, vegetables, etc.

examples.x: The grocery store had a wide selection of produce in the fresh section.
She prefers to buy organic produce from local farmers' markets.
The chef emphasized the use of seasonal produce in his dishes.
The restaurant sources its produce from nearby organic farms.
Fresh produce is essential for a healthy diet.

senses.definition: a small piece of food with a salty taste, that is often served at parties, etc.

examples.x: The party was a hit, thanks to the delicious array of savories served on the appetizer platter.
The chef prepared an assortment of mini savories to accompany the drinks at the reception.
For the potluck, he brought a tray of assorted savories, including cheese-stuffed olives and spicy crackers.
At the brunch, the guests enjoyed a mix of sweet pastries and a platter of assorted savories.
He prepared a tray of homemade savories for the book club meeting, including spiced nuts and stuffed mushrooms.

senses.definition: any sea creature that is eaten as food such as fish, shrimp, seaweed, and shellfish

examples.x: She's a seafood aficionado, specializing in preparing sushi and sashimi at home.
He enjoys dining on fresh seafood at the beachside restaurant, choosing from a variety of fish and shellfish.
He's on a seafood diet, preferring to eat only seafood dishes for their health benefits and delicious flavors.
The coastal town is known for its abundance of fresh seafood, attracting food enthusiasts from far and wide.
They enjoyed a seafood feast on the beach, with platters of shrimp, oysters, and grilled fish.

senses.definition: unappetizing and poorly prepared food, often in a liquid or semi-liquid form
the use of locally-sourced, sustainable, and traditional ingredients, as well as a slower pace of eating and appreciation for food culture
easy to consume in small, convenient portions
a flavorful cuisine rooted in African American culture
a food that is heavy and filling, but often uninspiring or dull
foods that are nutrient-rich and believed to have health benefits
food that contains little or no artificial substance and is considered healthy
any substance that can be used for consumption as food
food that is specially prepared for infants and young children to transition to solid foods
the type of food that makes one feel happy, because of containing a lot of carbohydrates or sugar, or reminds one of home
any type of food that is pre-prepared and can be cooked quickly
food that is quickly prepared and served, such as hamburgers, pizzas, etc.

examples.x: Fast food is convenient, but it's not the healthiest choice.
He grabbed some fast food on the way home from work.
His favorite fast food is fish and chips.
I try to avoid fast food, but sometimes I crave a burger.
We decided to get fast food instead of cooking tonight.

senses.definition: a colloquial term used to describe genetically modified or genetically engineered food
the type of food that is considered to be good for the body
unhealthy food, containing a lot of fat, sugar, etc.

examples.x: He decided to cut junk food from his diet for better health.
He replaced junk food with fresh fruits and vegetables in his diet.
He was craving junk food after a long day at work.
She decided to avoid junk food and started eating more home-cooked meals.
The party had a lot of junk food, so it was hard to stick to my diet.

senses.definition: new or unusual food that may require regulatory approval before being sold
a small bite of food
(of food) prepared according to Islamic law
(of food) prepared according to Jewish law

examples.x: The family hosted a kosher Passover Seder, with all the food prepared according to tradition.
He bought kosher wine for the Sabbath celebration.
They observed kosher guidelines during the holiday by avoiding mixing dairy and meat products in their meals.

senses.definition: ready-to-eat food or beverages that are sold by vendors in public places
things that people and animals eat, such as meat or vegetables

examples.x: He enjoyed trying new foods while traveling abroad.
I like to explore different cultures through their traditional foods.
She always tries to choose healthy and nutritious foods.
The children were hungry and eagerly devoured their food.

senses.definition: the food that we eat regularly during different times of day, such as breakfast, lunch, or dinner

examples.x: I cooked a delicious meal of grilled chicken with roasted vegetables.
On average, people consume three meals per day: breakfast, lunch, and dinner.
She ordered a takeaway meal of pizza and garlic bread for dinner.
She planned a romantic candlelit meal for their anniversary.
The meal was served buffet-style with a variety of dishes to choose from.

senses.definition: food that is essential to one's growth and health

examples.x: Fruits and vegetables are essential components of a healthy diet, providing valuable nutrition and vitamins to nourish the body.
As an athlete, he pays close attention to his nutrition, ensuring that his diet provides the fuel and nutrients needed for optimal performance.
The school implemented a nutrition education program to teach students about the importance of making healthy food choices and maintaining balanced diets.

senses.definition: the food that is needed in order to grow, live, and maintain health
the nourishing substances or food that provide the necessary nutrients and energy to sustain life
(typically plural) food and other items, typically household goods, that we buy at a supermarket such as eggs, flour, etc.

examples.x: She made a list of groceries to buy for the weekend.
I need to pick up some groceries on my way home from work.
The store offers online delivery services for groceries.
After the shopping trip, they unpacked the groceries in the kitchen.

senses.definition: a selection or variety of food or drink, often of a particular type or from a certain region

examples.x: The restaurant serves traditional Italian fare.
The café specializes in vegetarian fare.
Simple homemade fare can be the most comforting.
The cruise offered an elegant selection of seafood fare.
The festival featured a variety of street fare from different cultures.

senses.definition: a type of food or drink or other product that a place is known for because it is delivered in high quality

examples.x: The Italian restaurant's specialty is homemade pasta, made fresh daily by skilled chefs.
The bakery is famous for its specialty cakes, intricately decorated for weddings and birthdays.
They proudly serve their specialty coffee, sourced from small farms in South America.
The local brewery's specialty beer, a stout aged in bourbon barrels, has won several awards.
The spa's specialty treatment, a deep-tissue massage with aromatherapy, promotes relaxation and healing.

senses.definition: a food or a meal, especially in an informal or casual setting

examples.x: Let's grab some chow at the local diner after the movie.
I'm starving; do you know a good place for quick chow around here?
The cook prepared a delicious chow for the family gathering.
We'll have some homemade chow waiting for you when you arrive.
After a long day of hiking, everyone was eager to sit down and enjoy a hearty chow.

senses.definition: a mixture of various ingredients, often created with skill and creativity like a blend of flavors in a drink

examples.x: The chef's special concoction of herbs and spices gave the dish a unique flavor.
She sipped on a refreshing concoction of fruit juices and sparkling water.
The bartender surprised us with a creative concoction that combined unexpected ingredients.
Grandma's secret sauce was a delicious concoction that made every family dinner special.
Mom made a tasty concoction of fruits mixed together for a refreshing drink.

senses.definition: a food or meal

examples.x: Let's grab some eats at the new food truck downtown.
Every Friday night, our family tradition is to order takeout eats and enjoy a movie together.
We're having pizza for eats tonight, my treat!
I'm in the mood for Mexican eats tonight.
The picnic will have a variety of tasty eats for everyone to enjoy.

senses.definition: basic and hearty food

examples.x: In the college cafeteria, you can always find a variety of affordable grub options.
Let's order some comfort grub like pizza or burgers for a relaxed evening at home.
The diner is known for serving classic American grub, such as pancakes and bacon.
After a long hike, we sat around the campfire and enjoyed some delicious outdoor grub.
For the picnic, we packed a basket full of tasty grub, including sandwiches and fresh fruit.

senses.definition: a light snack or bite to eat, especially one enjoyed casually

examples.x: We sat in the park and shared a delightful nosh of cheese and crackers.
Before the movie, let's grab a quick nosh at the snack bar.
The party featured a variety of delicious noshes, from mini sandwiches to fruit platters.
At the coffee shop, you can always find a tempting selection of sweet and savory noshes.

senses.definition: the food served and consumed during a single meal or occasion, especially in a formal or festive setting

examples.x: The Thanksgiving repast included a variety of dishes, from roasted turkey to pumpkin pie.
At the wedding reception, the newlyweds treated their guests to an elaborate repast.
The family gathered around the dining table for a hearty repast on Sunday evenings.
As a tradition, the holiday season is marked by a festive repast shared among friends and family.
The picnic in the park turned into a delightful repast with sandwiches, fruits, and refreshing drinks.

senses.definition: a collection or variety of different food items available for consumption especially as a part of a meal or banquet

examples.x: The picnic basket was filled with viands like sandwiches, fruits, and cheese for everyone to enjoy.
The grocery store offered a diverse selection of viands, including fresh produce, meats, and bakery items.
During the camping trip, they packed a cooler with viands such as granola bars, nuts, and dried fruits.
The holiday feast featured a splendid spread of viands, from roasted turkey to decadent desserts.
In their restaurant, they take pride in offering viands that cater to different dietary preferences, ensuring a diverse dining experience.

senses.definition: a supply of feed or fodder for livestock or other animals

examples.x: The zookeepers carefully prepared a balanced provender mix for the animals, catering to their specific dietary needs.
The stable manager purchased high-quality provender to maintain the health of the racehorses.
Every day, the chicken farmer gives the birds a mix of seeds and grains as their provender.
She purchased provender from the store to provide nutritious food for her rabbits and guinea pigs.
The farmer stored a large quantity of provender in the barn to feed the livestock during the winter months.

senses.definition: food or provisions, especially when prepared for human consumption

examples.x: The restaurant proudly presented a menu filled with delicious victuals, ranging from appetizers to desserts.
During the winter months, the cellar was stocked with jars of pickled vegetables and other preserved victuals.
In preparation for the camping trip, they packed a cooler with a variety of victuals, including sandwiches and snacks.
Grandma always prepared comforting victuals, such as homemade soup and biscuits.
The picnic basket was filled with tasty victuals like sandwiches and fruits.

senses.definition: containing beneficial bacteria or microorganisms, often used to promote digestive health or balance within the body

examples.x: Sarah regularly consumes probiotic yogurt to support her digestive system.
Tom's doctor recommended a probiotic supplement to improve his gut health.
Jack's nutritionist suggested incorporating probiotic drinks into his daily routine to aid in digestion.
Emily drinks probiotic smoothies every morning to maintain a healthy balance of bacteria in her gut.
Tim's pharmacist recommended a probiotic medication to help with his antibiotic-associated diarrhea.

senses.definition: small, bite-sized food items that are designed to be eaten with the hands, without the need for utensils, and are often served at parties or gatherings
food that has had all its water removed to make it last longer

examples.x: Backpackers often rely on dehydrated food, like instant noodles and dried fruits, for lightweight and convenient meals.
During camping trips, hikers bring dehydrated food packets that can be easily rehydrated with hot water.
Some health-conscious individuals choose dehydrated food options, such as kale chips, for nutritious and convenient snacks.
Emergency preparedness kits often include dehydrated food items such as soups and pasta for long shelf life.
Dehydrated food, like powdered soup mixes and instant oatmeal, is commonly used for quick and easy meal preparation.

senses.definition: any edible substance that is not in a liquid or semi-liquid form and can be chewed and swallowed

examples.x: A balanced diet should include a combination of fruits, vegetables, grains, and other solid foods for optimal nutrition.
After fasting for a medical procedure, she eagerly looked forward to eating solid food again.
As an infant grows, they gradually transition from a milk-only diet to solid foods.
The doctor recommended introducing a variety of solid foods into the child's diet to support healthy development.
The patient's recovery progressed, and they were gradually allowed to reintroduce solid food into their diet after surgery.
